component.name: Google Drive Manager
component.summary: KDE-native Google Drive client with on-demand mount and offline pinning

description.p: A KDE Plasma 6 Google Drive client that gives Linux desktops the same experience the official Google Drive Desktop offers on Windows and macOS. Built on rclone.

ul.li: On-demand FUSE mount — your whole Drive appears as a regular folder; files download only when opened.
Per-folder and per-file "Keep always on this device" — right-click in Dolphin to pin items for offline access.
Reactive background sync via inotify — local edits propagate within seconds.
Dolphin overlay badges showing sync state (synced, syncing, error, online-only) on every file and folder.
Native Qt share dialog — add people by email, set Viewer/Commenter/Editor roles, send notification messages, revoke access; no browser hop required.
Multiple Google accounts side by side.
System tray icon with color-coded global sync state and per-account quick toggles.
Bisync conflict resolution UI for the inevitable two-sided edits.
Disk-usage panel showing real on-disk footprint per account.

screenshot.caption: Main application window with account status
Dolphin right-click menu with Google Drive integration
Native sharing dialog for adding people directly

description.p: Initial public release. Files-on-demand mount, folder + file pinning, in-app sharing, Dolphin overlay badges, multi-account support.
